mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher following rate cuts (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expansion and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60 days, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp month-over-month contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks and reduced factory utilization, causing short-term equity sell-offs as investors price in near-term earnings misses.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of cases following INDPRO declines, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading exceeding 2.5% triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly (K-S test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit growth, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or the series shows no statistical correlation with quarterly equity returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of low monetary policy rates signals capacity utilization approaching maximums, prompting short-term profit-taking and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate supply constraints.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below chance during low-rate environments.
